data.title: Sri Lanka Boutique Tours ↗
data.description: Designed and built the marketing site for a Tallinn-based boutique travel studio — a cinematic Vimeo hero, a curated tour catalogue, and a CMS-driven travel journal, localized across English, Estonian, and Russian.

data.title: BigWins iGaming platform
data.description: Primary frontend engineer for a crypto-first online casino. Built and maintained the design system and component library, localized into Japanese, and served as release manager.

data.title: Fishbrain — social app for 14M anglers
data.description: Built search-as-you-type for the main search (~30% faster), introduced Storybook visual testing into CI, and ran product A/B tests on the web platform.

data.title: Choreo cloud platform
data.description: Led frontend architecture for a cloud-native development platform, guided a team of 6, drove a company-wide testing strategy, and cut build time from 1.5 hours to ~20 minutes.

data.title: Kadira — performance monitoring
data.description: First employee on a monitoring platform for Meteor.js — grew it to 8,000+ users and 3,000+ paying customers, and helped productize React Storybook into a UI development environment.

data.title: Storybook Hub
data.description: A hosted UI development environment for React Storybook components, building both the front and back end.

data.title: BulletProof Meteor
data.description: A gamified tutorial site teaching how to build fast, efficient Meteor apps, with payments via Gumroad.

data.title: Comet Engine
data.description: Painless Meteor scaling and hosting, with one-click DigitalOcean deployments.

data.name: React Storybook
data.description: Contributed to the design and productization of Storybook — now the industry-standard UI development environment for components.

data.name: Kadira APM
data.description: Founding engineer on an end-to-end performance monitoring platform for Meteor.js, delivering real-time insights to thousands of apps.

data.name: Siyalu Browser
data.description: A free, open-source web browser with comprehensive Sinhala language support. Reached 100,000 downloads on Google Play.

data.name: Kichibichiya
data.description: A friendly Twitter client for Android with full Sinhala language support, built on Twidere.

data.name: Wadan Sewuma
data.description: An English→Sinhala dictionary app for Android, with camera word capture for instant lookups. Nominated Best Android App at Etisalat AppZone Champions 2012.

data.name: QSChrome
data.description: A Sinhala phonetic typing keyboard for Google Chrome — the first of its kind, before Google Input Tools shipped one.

data.name: Censitip
data.description: A Sinhala pop-up dictionary extension for Chrome — instant definitions on hover, anywhere on the web.

data.name: Sinhala Word Cloud
data.description: The first Sinhala word cloud, generated with D3 from a full book’s text and used on the cover of “Economic Strategies Appropriate for Sri Lanka.”

data.name: Buzzradio
data.description: Community-driven online radio on Google App Engine, with real-time chat and Ogg audio streaming.

data.name: Hathmaluwa
data.description: A blog aggregator for Sinhala, Tamil & English blogs, plus a companion Android reader.

data.name: OLPC Workshops
data.description: Volunteer workshops for One Laptop Per Child, bringing low-cost laptops to schoolchildren.

data.title: Fight Club — Overall Winner
data.issuer: Node.js Knockout · Nov 2015 · with Kadira Inc.
data.description: A real-time, webcam-based fighting game built in a 48-hour global hackathon — powered by WebRTC and motion detection, and inspired by the film. Judged the overall winner against roughly 400 teams worldwide.

data.title: KO-Train — Overall Winner & People's Choice
data.issuer: CMB.js Hackathon, Colombo · Apr 2014 · with Kadira Inc.
data.description: A train-delay notification system built with Meteor.js and Android. Took both the overall winner and People's Choice awards at Colombo's JS hackathon.

data.title: The Big Wall — Top 5 finish
data.issuer: Node.js Knockout · Nov 2016 · with Kadira Inc.
data.description: A virtual wall spanning the US–Mexico border where anyone could draw collaborative graffiti in real time. Placed 5th overall among roughly 400 competing teams.

data.title: Open Comment Box — Top 6 finish
data.issuer: Node.js Knockout · Nov 2013 · with Kadira Inc.
data.description: An open-source, self-hostable real-time commenting platform — a Disqus alternative you can run anywhere. Placed 6th overall among roughly 400 competing teams.
